title: andira anthelmia
intro: Andira anthelmia, also known as the 'Guatambu' or 'Pau Ferro', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America. The medicinal properties of Andira anthelmia are primarily attributed to its heartwood, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of using Andira anthelmia include the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and relief from parasitic infections. The plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, with herbal preparations typically involving the heartwood being steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
